title: Topology Migration — org_nodes / org_node_roles / org_node_view_state → vector_artefacts
status_note: Substantially complete on handler/route side as of 2026-05-14. topology.Service constructed at main.go L378 as `topology.New(pool, vaPool).WithNotifier(...)`. Per the service doc-comment (internal/topology/service.go:154-174): EVERY topology read/write goes to vaPool; the legacy mmff_vector pool is retained ONLY for membership/auth lookups (PoolWorkspaceLookup adapter). Route registered on both /_site/topology (main.go L1040) and /samantha/v2/topology. ETL state of org_nodes / roles_org_nodes / org_node_view_state mmff_vector → vector_artefacts is unverified and frontend caller migration to apiV2/apiSite is unverified.
scope: Migrate the three topology tables (org_nodes, roles_org_nodes, org_node_view_state) from mmff_vector to vector_artefacts. org_nodes is a self-referential tree (parent_id self-FK) with subscription-scoped tenancy and spatial canvas coordinates. roles_org_nodes is the cross-table that links RBAC roles to org nodes. org_node_view_state stores per-user canvas viewport state. No equivalent tables exist in vector_artefacts today — all three must be created via new artefacts_schema migrations. Once migrated, /topology is registered under /samantha/v2 and removed from v1.
value: Unblocks /topology from v1. The topology canvas (PLA-0006) is the organisational modelling surface — having it served from vector_artefacts aligns it with the rest of the workspace-scoped data model and removes the last structural/organisational table from mmff_vector.
implementation_plan: Task 1: Schema design — define org_nodes, roles_org_nodes, org_node_view_state in vector_artefacts. Key decisions: tenancy via workspace_id (replacing subscription_id); self-referential parent_id FK within vector_artefacts; spatial fields (x, y, width, height) retained as-is; roles_org_nodes cross-references roles table (already in vector_artefacts per PLA-0007 if roles were migrated, or retain cross-DB soft FK).
Task 3: ETL — migrate org_nodes from mmff_vector to vector_artefacts; preserve tree structure (parent_id must be remapped to new IDs or retained if UUIDs match).
Task 4: ETL — migrate roles_org_nodes; resolve role_id cross-DB reference (roles table may still be in mmff_vector or moved by PLA-0007).
Task 5: ETL — migrate org_node_view_state (per-user viewport — low-risk, can be reset on cutover if mapping is complex).
Task 6: Rewrite orgdesign.Service to query vaPool; tenancy gate via workspace_id; membership check against mmff_vector retained if roles still cross-DB.
Task 7: Register /topology under /samantha/v2 in main.go; remove /samantha/v1 registration.
Task 8: Update frontend callers — api('/topology') → apiV2('/topology').
Task 9: Update openapi-v2.yaml + openapi.yaml; update c_c_v1_v2_cutover.md and PLA-0030.
areas_impacted: Backend: backend/cmd/server/main.go — v2 registration, v1 deregistration

artefacts_schema migrations 027/028/029
work_item_backlog.note: Topology tables exist in vector_artefacts (renamed to topology_nodes / topology_role_grants / topology_view_state per service doc-comment)

work_item_backlog.note: Package now `topology` (not `orgdesign`); all reads/writes use vaPool per backend/internal/topology/service.go

work_item_backlog.title: Register /topology under /samantha/v2; remove v1 registration
work_item_backlog.note: Registered on /_site/topology (main.go L1040) and /samantha/v2/topology; /samantha/v1 block entirely removed under PLA-0039

work_item_backlog.title: Update openapi specs + cutover register + PLA-0030
work_item_backlog.note: c_c_v1_v2_cutover.md reconciled 2026-05-14; openapi paperwork tracked under PLA-0030 T9

acceptance_criteria.criterion: GET /samantha/v2/topology returns org tree sourced from vector_artefacts with correct node hierarchy
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: curl smoke test; tree depth and parent_id relationships validated against migrated data

acceptance_criteria.criterion: No frontend callers reference api('/topology') — check_callers.py reports 0

risks.risk: org_nodes is a self-referential tree — UUID remapping during ETL must preserve parent_id integrity; a single mis-mapped UUID breaks the entire tree
risks.mitigation: ETL retains original UUIDs where possible (INSERT with explicit id); post-ETL verify all parent_id values exist in the migrated set

risks.risk: roles_org_nodes references roles table — if roles still live in mmff_vector at migration time (PLA-0007 not yet complete for vaPool), the FK becomes a cross-DB soft FK
risks.mitigation: Coordinate with PLA-0007 completion timeline; if roles not yet in vaPool, use soft FK (UUID column, no PG FK constraint) and document as tech debt

risks.risk: org_node_view_state is per-user viewport data — mapping user_id across DBs may be lossy
risks.mitigation: Accept viewport state reset on cutover as low-impact; document in release notes
